definition: if food is bland, it does not have much flavour
example: his cooking is always rather bland

definition: if food is filling, you only have to eat a small amount to feel full
example: a filling soup

definition: if you grate food, you cut it into a lot of very thin strips
example: grate the carrots and onion very finely
baked potato with grated cheese
a cheese grater

definition: if food is juicy, it has a lot of natural juice or liquid in it
example: the meat was tender and juicy
the juice was running down his chin

definition: if you mash food, especially potatoes, you crush them until they become smooth
example: mash the potatoes with butter and milk
serve with rice or mashed potatoes
sausages and mash

definition: if you slice something, you use a knife to cut thin pieces of it
example: slice the ham thinly
a packet of sliced bread
a slice of bread

definition: food that is sour has a strong taste that is not at all sweet, like a lemon
example: sour cherry juice

definition: if you steam vegetables or fish, you cook them using only the steam from boiling water instead of putting them into the boiling water itself
example: do you want to steam the courgettes, or shall we bake them?
it comes with salad or steamed vegetables

definition: food that is stuffed is cooked with a mixture of other foods inside it
example: balls of mashed potato stuffed with beef, raisins, and olives
itвАЩs quite difficult to stuff a pigeon
a herb and onion stuffing

definition: if you are adventurous, you like trying lots of different and exciting things
example: heвАЩs not a very adventurous eater
a bland and unadventurous dish

definition: if you cope, you deal successfully with a difficult situation
example: they couldnвАЩt cope with the food they were given

definition: if you get hold of something, you succeed in getting it for yourself by gripping it firmly so that no one else can have it
example: he got hold of the gun and pointed it at the robber

definition: your host is the person whose home you are staying in, or the person who has invited you to an event such as a party
example: our host welcomed us at the door
we hosted a dinner for several of our neighbours

definition: if you integrate, you start to belong in a new place or situation and are accepted as part of it
example: it takes time to integrate into a new country
they quickly became integrated into the social and economic life of the village

definition: integration is the fact of belonging in a new place or situation and being accepted there
example: his integration into his new school took a long time

definition: if something is a long way off or a long time off, it hasnвАЩt happened yet or is still some distance away
example: full integration is some way off yet

definition: if something represents another thing, it stands as a symbol of that other thing, often because it has many of the qualities of that other thing
example: these places represent everything their owners miss about their home country

definition: if you split something, you divide it into smaller parts which are usually equal
example: letвАЩs just split the bill (letвАЩs all pay the same amount)

definition: if you say you are starving, you mean you are very hungry. You also say people are starving when they are dying because they do not have enough food to eat
example: I was starving by the time we got home
if we donвАЩt send help, a million people will starve to death there

definition: sweat is water that comes out through your skin because you are hot, for example after you have done exercise. If you are sweaty, you have sweat on your skin. If your clothes are sweaty they are slightly wet with sweat
example: it smells like sweaty socks
sweat was running down his back
sweat was running down his back

definition: if you say that itвАЩs no wonder something is the case, you mean that the reason for it is obvious
example: with food like that, itвАЩs no wonder there are so many foreign restaurants here

definition: if somewhere is bare, it looks empty and is not filled or covered with anything
example: the room was cold and bare

definition: if you demand something, you say very forcefully that you want it and expect it to be brought to you or given to you
example: I demand to know what is going on here!
his demand was met with a direct refusal to cooperate

definition: if somewhere is deserted, there is no one there
example: the station was deserted

definition: if you say that someone deserves something, you think it is right for them to get it, either as a reward for something good that they have done, or as a punishment for something bad that they have done
example: he doesnвАЩt deserve the extra money

definition: a gang is a group of people who spend a lot of time together, often for criminal reasons
example: an organised criminal gang

definition: an instant is an extremely short length of time. If you say that you would do something in an instant, you mean that you would do it immediately without waiting at all
example: IвАЩd walk out in an instant if he treated me like that
the film was an instant success
I had a glass of water and instantly felt better

definition: you use literally to emphasise that you are using a word or words in their exact meaning, even though it might be surprising. People also use the word literally just to emphasize what they are saying, but many teachers think this use is wrong
example: literally at that exact moment I saw a rat going into the kitchen
the literal meaning of the word вАЬYogaвАЭ is вАЬunionвАЭ

transcription: l Кk ЋИa Кt ЋМ…Щ Кv…Щ(r)
definition: if a building or window looks out over something or onto something, it has a view of that place
example: our hotel room looks out over the sea

definition: organic food has been produced without the use of artificial chemicals or fertilizers
example: all our meat and vegetables are organic
all our vegetables are grown organically

definition: something or someone that is outstanding is extremely impressive
example: the service was outstanding

definition: if a place or object is packed, it is full of people or things
example: the restaurant is always packed

definition: a portion of food is an amount that is served to one person at a meal
example: you need four meatballs per portion

definition: If you say that a place is posh, you mean that it looks expensive and is probably used by rich and fashionable people
example: Jenny took me to a posh restaurant for my birthday

definition: if you present something in a certain way, that is how you arrange it and place it in front the person you are giving it to
example: they present their dishes with style and taste
the food was tasty, but the presentation was poor

definition: a single person or thing is just one. You say not a single or not one single person or thing to emphasise that there was not one person or thing somewhere
example: I didnвАЩt see a single person I knew in the restaurant

definition: if you spit something out of your mouth, you force it out
example: he began coughing and spitting blood

definition: if you say that you will stick with something, you mean that you will continue doing it or having it, and do not want to exchange it or do something different
example: IвАЩll stick with what IвАЩve got

definition: In chemistry, acids are substances that have a pH value of less than 7. Strong acid can burn holes in metal. Acid soil or conditions have a certain amount of acid in them
example: to grow well, cranberries like acid conditions and sand
a bottle of sulphuric acid
the oceans are becoming more acidic

definition: something that is adequate is enough in quantity or quality for a particular purpose
example: do you have an adequate supply of water?
the factory was not adequately equipped to meet the demand

definition: a crop is a plant such as wheat or corn that is grown by farmers in order to sell it
example: a frost can destroy the crop

definition: if something floats, it stays on the surface of a liquid and does not sink
example: the berries then float to the surface

definition: to harvest crops means to pick them from the fields or trees where they are growing when they are ripe and ready to be eaten or turned into food
example: boys came out of school to help the farmers harvest their crops
the weather stayed fine throughout the harvest (the time when the crops were being harvested)

definition: if you inherit goods or property, it becomes yours because someone has left instructions that you should have it after they die. If you inherit characteristics from your parents, you have those characteristics because of the genes you share with them
example: if he has no children, who will inherit?
he expected to receive a large inheritance on the death of his father

definition: if you keep an eye on a situation, you make sure you know what is happening so that you can take action at the right moment or if something goes wrong or needs attention
example: they have to keep an eye on the weather because a frost can destroy the crop

definition: if you remove something, you take it away from where it is, or you take it away from the other things that are with it
example: they remove the berries which then float to the surface
removal of the rear seat is easy

definition: sand is the stuff you find on lots of beaches or in deserts. It is like a fine powder and is made up of very tiny pieces of rock
example: to grow well, cranberries like acid conditions and sand
a sandy beach

definition: a supply of something is an amount of it that is available for use
example: do you have an adequate supply of water?

definition: the surface of something is the top of it that faces upwards
example: the berries then float to the surface

definition: your best dish is the dish that you can cook better than any other, or the one you enjoy eating most
example: what's your best dish?

definition: a fussy eater is someone who is difficult to please about food and refuses to eat many things
example: my little brother is such a fussy eater

definition: if you deep fry food, you cook it by completely covering it in hot oil
example: deep fry the chicken until golden brown

definition: if you stir-fry food, you cook small pieces of it very quickly in a small amount of very hot oil while stirring constantly
example: stir-fry the vegetables for two minutes

definition: if you roast food, you cook it in an oven or over a fire using dry heat
example: roast the chicken for an hour

definition: if you boil food, you cook it in very hot water
example: boil the potatoes for twenty minutes

definition: food that is spicy has a strong, hot taste because of the spices used to cook it
example: I love spicy Thai food

definition: food that is fattening makes you fat if you eat too much of it
example: chocolate cake is very fattening

definition: food that is greasy contains or is covered in too much fat or oil
example: the fish and chips were really greasy

definition: foreign food is food that comes from or is typical of a country that is not your own
example: she loves trying foreign food when she travels

definition: if you talk someone through something, you explain it to them in detail, often step by step
example: can you talk me through the recipe?

definition: raisins are small dried grapes
example: add a handful of raisins to the mixture

definition: you say this to tell someone that a description of food makes you want to eat it
example: a chocolate cake with raspberry filling вАФ it sounds very tasty

definition: a specialist shop sells only one particular type of product
example: you can buy fresh pasta at the specialist shop round the corner

definition: if you are bound to do something, it is very likely or certain that you will do it
example: if you try enough new dishes, you're bound to find one you love

definition: a wide range of things is a large number of different types of them
example: the restaurant offers a wide range of vegetarian dishes

definition: something that is off-putting makes you feel dislike or lose interest in something
example: the smell was quite off-putting

definition: mould is a soft grey, green or black substance that grows on old food or in damp conditions
example: there was mould growing on the bread

definition: veggie food is food that does not contain meat or fish
example: the cafe has a good selection of veggie food

definition: chopsticks are a pair of thin sticks used, especially in Asian countries, for eating food
example: he can eat rice with chopsticks really well

definition: a host family is a family that a student or visitor stays with, usually while studying abroad
example: she lived with a host family during her year in France

definition: if you do something beforehand, you do it before a particular event or time
example: we booked the table beforehand

definition: a bar of chocolate is a solid rectangular piece of chocolate
example: she ate a whole bar of chocolate

definition: something that is enormous is extremely large in size or amount
example: they served an enormous portion of pasta

definition: you say this when you dislike something so much that you cannot bear to experience or deal with it
example: the food was so spicy, I couldn't stand it

definition: something that is weird is strange and unusual
example: the combination of flavours sounded weird

definition: ready meals are meals that have already been cooked and only need to be heated before eating
example: he lives on ready meals because he doesn't have time to cook

definition: if you work full time, you work the whole of each normal working week rather than only part of it
example: both parents work full time

definition: you use generally speaking to introduce a statement that is true in most cases
example: generally speaking, British food has a bad reputation abroad

definition: a special occasion is an event that is important or celebrated, such as a birthday or anniversary
example: we only eat out on special occasions

definition: if you leave a tip, you leave extra money for someone such as a waiter as a way of rewarding them for good service
example: it's normal to leave tips in restaurants in the US

definition: home-style cooking is food prepared in a simple way, similar to how it would be made at home rather than in a professional way
example: the restaurant is famous for its home-style cooking

definition: if you overestimate something, you think it is bigger, better or more important than it really is
example: I overestimated how much food we would need

definition: if you overcook food, you cook it for too long so that it is spoiled
example: don't overcook the pasta or it will go soft

definition: if someone overcharges you, they make you pay too much money for something
example: the restaurant overcharged us for the wine

definition: if you overeat, you eat more food than you need
example: it's easy to overeat at a buffet

word: I would explode if I ate any more
transcription: a…™ w Кd …™kЋИspl…Щ Кd …™f a…™ e…™t ЋИeni m…ФЋР(r)
definition: you say this to emphasise that you have eaten so much that you cannot possibly eat anything else
example: no more, thanks вАФ I would explode if I ate any more!

definition: a canteen is a place, for example in a school, office or factory, where food is served to the people who work or study there
example: we usually have lunch in the staff canteen

definition: a famine is a situation in which large numbers of people have little or no food for a long time
example: the drought led to a severe famine

definition: obesity is the condition of being extremely overweight
example: childhood obesity has increased in recent years

definition: nutrition is the process of providing or getting the food necessary for health and growth
example: good nutrition is important for children's development
